Container.Name: StreamBox
Container.Overview:
StreamBox - Virtual 1080p60 desktop that streams to any RTMP server. Uses Intel iGPU (VA-API) for hardware encoding with near-zero CPU usage. Includes Chromium (with Widevine DRM), VLC, and a stream control panel. Access the desktop via web browser (noVNC), native VNC client, or SSH.
IMPORTANT: Requires Intel iGPU passthrough (--device=/dev/dri:/dev/dri) and --shm-size=2g for browser stability. These are set in Extra Parameters.
Tested stable for 6+ hours continuous streaming on i9-9900K (UHD 630).
Container.Description: StreamBox streams a virtual desktop to an RTMP server using Intel VA-API hardware encoding. Features Chromium with Widevine DRM support, VLC, and a built-in stream control panel. Access via browser (noVNC), VNC client, or SSH. See README for full documentation.

Config.Name: noVNC Web Port
Config.Description: Web-based VNC access. Open http://SERVER_IP:6080 in your browser to see and control the desktop. This is the easiest way to interact with StreamBox.

Config.Name: VNC Port
Config.Description: Native VNC client access (TightVNC, RealVNC, etc). Better performance than noVNC for intensive use. Connect to SERVER_IP:5900 with your VNC client.

Config.Name: SSH Port
Config.Description: SSH access for CLI management. Connect with: ssh root@SERVER_IP -p 2222. Use for monitoring (stream-status), restarting (stream-restart), and checking logs.

Config.Name: RTMP Server URL
Config.Description: Your RTMP ingest URL. Examples: rtmp://192.168.1.100:1935/live (local server), rtmp://a.rtmp.youtube.com/live2 (YouTube). Do NOT include the stream key here -- that goes in the next field.

Config.Name: Stream Key
Config.Description: Your stream key or stream name. This is appended to the RTMP URL to form the full path (e.g. rtmp://server/live/YOUR_KEY). Get this from your streaming platform or RTMP server config.

Config.Name: Video Encoder
Config.Description: BEST CHOICE: 'qsv' uses Intel VA-API hardware encoding (near-zero CPU, requires iGPU passthrough via --device=/dev/dri:/dev/dri). Use 'x264' only if you have no Intel GPU -- this uses software encoding and will consume significant CPU. The 'qsv' label is legacy; it actually uses h264_vaapi with CQP rate control.

Config.Name: VNC Password
Config.Description: Password for VNC access (both noVNC web and native VNC clients). Change this from the default for security.

Config.Name: SSH Password
Config.Description: Password for SSH root access (ssh root@SERVER_IP -p 2222). Change this from the default for security. You can also add SSH keys to /root/.ssh/authorized_keys inside the container.

Config.Name: Display Width
Config.Description: Virtual display width in pixels. 1920 for 1080p, 1280 for 720p. Must match your RTMP server's expected input resolution. Changing this requires container restart.

Config.Name: Display Height
Config.Description: Virtual display height in pixels. 1080 for 1080p, 720 for 720p. Must match your RTMP server's expected input resolution. Changing this requires container restart.

Config.Name: Stream FPS
Config.Description: Stream framerate. BEST CHOICE: 60 for smooth video/motion, 30 if bandwidth is limited. VA-API hardware encoding handles 60fps with near-zero CPU. Note: your RTMP server must accept and remux at the same FPS to avoid microstutter.

Config.Name: Video Bitrate (x264 only)
Config.Description: ONLY used when ENCODER=x264 (software encoding). Ignored for VA-API/QSV -- VA-API uses CQP (constant quality) at QP 26 which produces ~6-12 Mbps depending on content. To change VA-API quality, edit the -qp value in start-stream.sh (lower=better, 18-28 range).

Config.Name: Encoder Preset (x264 only)
Config.Description: ONLY used when ENCODER=x264. Fastest to best quality: ultrafast, superfast, veryfast, faster, fast, medium. BEST CHOICE: 'veryfast' balances quality and CPU. Use 'ultrafast' if CPU is maxed out. Ignored when using VA-API hardware encoding.

Config.Name: Audio Bitrate
Config.Description: AAC audio bitrate. BEST CHOICE: 128k for good quality stereo. 96k is fine for speech-only content, 192k for high-quality music. Higher values use marginally more bandwidth.

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Container timezone. Affects log timestamps and browser time display. Examples: America/New_York, America/Chicago, Europe/London, Asia/Dubai, Asia/Tokyo. Full list: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones

Config.Name: Config Storage
Config.Description: Persistent storage for container data (browser profiles, VNC config, etc). Survives container rebuilds. IMPORTANT: This grows over time with browser cache -- periodically check size.

Config.Name: Media Files (Optional)
Config.Description: Optional: Mount your media library for VLC playback inside the desktop. Mounted read-only to prevent accidental modifications. Leave empty if not needed.
